note: Crafts of The Descent/The Ascent (demonic_angelic axis). DESCENT is the demonic-pole craft of the Abyssal Choir — appetite, bargain, the honesty about hunger the pious lack; ASCENT is the angelic-pole craft of the Seraphic Orders — mercy, judgment, luminous certainty. Neither is 'evil' or 'good' in the flat sense: the Choir's honesty about want is a real virtue and the Orders' certainty is a real danger. The pure loci (The Maw, The Choir-Height) are cult-held. Authored by Aevi, SNG-054.

abilities.name: Descent
abilities.narrationHints: Something in the room admits what it wants, and the admitting has teeth.
abilities.description: The demonic-pole mastery: appetite named, bargains struck, the refusal to pretend that want is not the engine. Not evil — the honest hunger.
abilities.notFor: Pretending you do not want what you want (that is the Orders' failure, not the Choir's); mercy without price; anything that requires you to be innocent.

tree.name: The Named Want
tree.grants: Name what a person actually wants — not what they claim, not what they should — and speak it aloud so they hear it.
tree.cannot: Naming a want is not granting it. Some people will hate you forever for the naming.

tree.name: The Bargain
tree.grants: Strike a binding bargain: both sides get what they truly want, and both sides pay what it truly costs.
tree.cannot: The Bargain is exactly fair, which is the horror of it. It will not protect someone from the price of their own wanting.

tree.name: The Deep Descent
tree.grants: Descend into a person, a place, or an institution and surface with its true engine — the appetite it is actually running on, however it dresses.
tree.cannot: What you surface with, you now carry. The Choir's deepest are unable to see anyone as anything but their hunger, and are usually right, and are unbearable.

abilities.name: Appetite-Sense
abilities.narrationHints: You can feel the shape of what they are hungry for before they say a word.
abilities.description: Sense the true want under the presented want — the first gift of the Descent.
abilities.notFor: Reading facts (that is Verity); reading feeling (that is Pathos). This reads HUNGER.

tree.name: Appetite-Sense
tree.grants: Feel what a person actually wants, beneath what they are asking for.
tree.cannot: It reads want, not intent. What they will DO about the want is a different craft.

tree.name: The Hollow Read
tree.grants: Feel the want a person is refusing to admit to themselves — the one doing the most work.
tree.cannot: Knowing the want they deny is a weapon, and it is very hard not to use it.

tree.name: The Whole Hunger
tree.grants: Read the appetite of a crowd, a faction, a city — what the whole thing is actually reaching for.
tree.cannot: A crowd's hunger is not the sum of its people's. It is stupider and much stronger.

abilities.name: The Offered Price
abilities.narrationHints: The price is named plainly, and it is worse for being fair.
abilities.description: Name the true price of a thing someone wants — exactly, honestly, with nothing hidden.
abilities.notFor: Lying about the price (Falsecraft's country, and the Choir despises it); forcing the taking.

tree.name: The Offered Price
tree.grants: Name what a thing will truly cost the person who wants it. They may still take it.
tree.cannot: Naming the price does not stop them. It only means they cannot say they were not told.

tree.name: The Held Price
tree.grants: The price, once named and accepted, BINDS — it will be paid, in the coin named, whatever they do afterward.
tree.cannot: You are now the one who holds it. Collecting is not optional, even when you have come to love them.

tree.name: The True Cost
tree.grants: Name what a course of action will ultimately cost — not the ledger price, the real one, years out.
tree.cannot: People do not believe true costs. The Choir's oldest grief is being right and ignored.

abilities.name: Ascent
abilities.narrationHints: The light here does not warm. It measures.
abilities.description: The angelic-pole mastery: mercy given, judgment pronounced, the luminous certainty that both heals and terrifies. Not good in the flat sense — certainty is a danger.
abilities.notFor: Doubt (the Orders have very little); mercy that costs the giver nothing; being wrong gracefully.

tree.name: The Measuring Eye
tree.grants: See a person's moral weight — what they have done, what it cost others, what they carry.
tree.cannot: It measures the deed, not the person. The Orders forget this distinction constantly and catastrophically.

tree.name: The Mercy
tree.grants: Grant a real absolution — lift a weight a person has carried, genuinely, and they will feel it leave.
tree.cannot: Mercy given is mercy TAKEN ON. The weight does not vanish; it moves to you.

tree.name: The Judgment
tree.grants: Pronounce a judgment that BINDS — it is true, everyone present knows it is true, and it does not come undone.
tree.cannot: If you are wrong, the judgment binds anyway. The Seraphic Orders' history is a long argument about that sentence.

abilities.name: The Weighing Look
abilities.narrationHints: You are being looked at by something that is adding you up.
abilities.description: See the moral weight a person carries — the first gift of the Ascent.
abilities.notFor: Reading want (Appetite-Sense); reading truth (Verity). This reads WEIGHT — deed and cost.

tree.name: The Weighing Look
tree.grants: See roughly what a person carries — the harm done, the harm borne.
tree.cannot: It sees weight, not story. A heavy person may be heavy for good reasons.

tree.name: The Clear Ledger
tree.grants: See the specifics: what was done, to whom, and whether it was ever answered for.
tree.cannot: Seeing the ledger tempts you to collect on it. That is the Orders' besetting sin.

tree.name: The Unblinking Measure
tree.grants: Weigh anyone, at any distance, without their consent or knowledge.
tree.cannot: Nobody survives being weighed forever. Including you, and the Orders do it to their own.

abilities.name: The Shielding Word
abilities.narrationHints: A word is spoken and the blow simply does not arrive.
abilities.description: Interpose a spoken certainty between harm and a person — the Seraphic ward, which is why the Orders are loved despite everything.
abilities.notFor: Warding what deserves to land (the Orders will not, and this is either integrity or arrogance depending on who you ask).

tree.name: The Shielding Word
tree.grants: Ward one person from one harm with a spoken certainty.
tree.cannot: You must actually believe it. A hollow word wards nothing, and everyone can hear the hollow.

tree.name: The Standing Ward
tree.grants: The ward holds over a place and a people, for as long as you keep speaking for them.
tree.cannot: You must keep speaking. And you must keep meaning it, which is harder.

tree.name: The Unbroken Word
tree.grants: A ward that holds after you have gone — a word spoken so wholly it keeps standing on its own.
tree.cannot: It stands on what you meant when you said it. If you change, it does not. Sister Alder's ward is of this kind, and she has thought about that a great deal.

abilities.name: The Appetite Strike
abilities.narrationHints: She didn't hit the person. She hit what the person was protecting.
abilities.description: Strike at a person's hunger — not the body, but what they've been keeping safe by not admitting it.

tree.name: The Unguarded Want
tree.grants: Strike at what a person most doesn't want to lose — they are fortified against the ordinary attack but have no guard on what they want.

tree.name: Named and Struck
tree.grants: Name the appetite and hit it — not the body, what the body has been protecting. The wound is real and lands where they cannot wall it out.

tree.name: The Thing They Would Not Name
tree.grants: Hit what they kept unnamed. They will know what was struck. They will not be the same on the other side of it.

abilities.name: The Consumed Wound
abilities.narrationHints: The price is paid. So is the wound.
abilities.description: Offer a wound or pain to the deep — it will be taken. What the abyss eats doesn't come back, but neither does the hurt.

tree.name: Give It Down
tree.grants: Offer a wound, pain, or carried weight to the deep — the abyss will take it. The wound goes with it.

tree.name: Consumed Entirely
tree.grants: Not healed in the ordinary sense — gone; the abyss ate it and what remains is the person without what they were carrying.

tree.name: The Transfer
tree.grants: Take a wound from another into yourself and offer it down. You carry it briefly; then you don't; then neither does anyone.

abilities.name: The Dread Mantle
abilities.narrationHints: What she'd seen was still on her. Things that hadn't seen it backed away.
abilities.description: Carry what you've been through as armor — the weight of where you've been deters what would prey on the ordinary.

tree.name: Let It Show
tree.grants: Let what you carry show — the depth of where you've been. Things that prey on the vulnerable find another target.

tree.name: The Real Weight
tree.grants: The dread around you is a genuine deterrent — not fear you project, weight you carry that others feel before they decide to act.

tree.name: It Precedes You
tree.grants: The mantle arrives before you do. Those who would act against you are navigating something they don't want to examine, and most choose not to.

abilities.name: The Called Form
abilities.narrationHints: She reached down and pulled something up. It stood there, not quite a thing.
abilities.description: Call something from the deep — the fear beneath the anger, the hunger beneath the pride — and give it form.

tree.name: The Surfaced Want
tree.grants: Summon the underside of a thing — the fear beneath the anger, the hunger beneath the pride — and give it momentary form in the room.

tree.name: Called and Held
tree.grants: Call a form from the deep that serves a purpose — an appetite made briefly real, a fear given body — and it holds until it is dismissed.

tree.name: What You Called Is Yours
tree.grants: What you call up holds, acts in the world, and is yours to direct until you release it. It is not docile. It is yours.

abilities.name: The Descent Path
abilities.narrationHints: The way down is always there. You just have to be willing to look for it.
abilities.description: Find or make the passage to the below — the literal underground, the hidden layer, the place others don't know to reach.

tree.name: The Way Down
tree.grants: Find the passage to the below — the underground route, the hidden bottom of a space, the layer the map doesn't show.

tree.name: The Deep Road
tree.grants: Travel through the below — beneath sight, beneath light, along the layer others don't know how to reach.

tree.name: Guide the Descent
tree.grants: Take others down the descent path and bring them back up. The route is yours to walk now and to share.

abilities.name: The Choir Sustains
abilities.narrationHints: She was running out. Then she wasn't. Something below was still going.
abilities.description: Sustain through communion with the choir — when you begin to fail, the deep holds what the above has exhausted.

tree.name: The Deep Holds
tree.grants: When you begin to run out, the communion sustains — something from below keeps going what above has exhausted.

tree.name: Sustaining the Group
tree.grants: Draw from the depth to carry your party through the hardest part — the energy isn't yours, but it is available to those who have been where you have been.

tree.name: The Unexhausted Deep
tree.grants: The communion sustains indefinitely in conditions of deprivation — as long as the connection holds, you and yours hold.

abilities.name: The Judged Strike
abilities.narrationHints: The blow wasn't just a blow. It said something.
abilities.description: Strike with the weight of judgment — the blow carries condemnation; the target knows what it means.

tree.name: The Warranted Blow
tree.grants: Strike with the weight of judgment — the blow lands harder because it is warranted; the target knows it.

tree.name: Named in the Strike
tree.grants: A judged blow harms and reveals — what they have done is named in the moment of impact; all present understand the context.

tree.name: Hierarchy's Strike
tree.grants: Strike with the full authority of the order — the blow is both physical and pronouncement; what was done is named, weighed, and answered.

abilities.name: The Raised Form
abilities.narrationHints: She didn't just build it. She raised it. There's a difference.
abilities.description: Make through seraphic authority — what is raised embodies proper order and organizes what surrounds it.

tree.name: The Ordered Thing
tree.grants: Make something that embodies proper order — a structure, a space, an object whose form holds the hierarchy clearly.

tree.name: The Organizing Made
tree.grants: Raise a form that organizes what surrounds it — the made thing sets the tone for the space it occupies.

tree.name: What Should Exist
tree.grants: Summon the form a thing should have and instantiate it — the building that should stand, the arrangement that should be — made real by the authority to say what is proper.

abilities.name: The Ascent Path
abilities.narrationHints: The way up is always there for those who are meant to go.
abilities.description: Ascend past what should be a physical limit — and open the ascent path for others.

tree.name: Past the Limit
tree.grants: Ascend past what should be a physical barrier — scale what isn't scalable, reach the height that closes off the ordinary approach.

tree.name: The Opened Way
tree.grants: Show the ascent path and others can follow — the route up that wasn't there before is there now for those you lead.

tree.name: The Choir-Height
tree.grants: Travel the ascent as a road — any height, any approach — and carry your party with you to wherever the way up leads.

abilities.name: The Sustained Order
abilities.narrationHints: She wasn't doing it alone. She hadn't been for a while.
abilities.description: Sustain through participation in the hierarchy — the order holds those who hold to it.

tree.name: The Order Holds
tree.grants: Draw from the hierarchy to go on — the seraphic who holds their place in the order does not run out alone.

tree.name: What the Order Provides
tree.grants: Sustain your party through the hierarchy's resources — not comfort but genuine sustaining authority; what the order gives, you carry some of.

tree.name: Part of What the Order Can Do
tree.grants: In the hardest conditions, the seraphic who remains part of the order remains part of what the order can do — which is more than any single body can.
